rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of 2614 Sacramento Street, Llc, To Cancel Release The Mechanics Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 18, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER 2614 SACRAMENTO STREET, LLC Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of 2614 Sacramento Street, Llc, To Cancel Release The Mechanics Lien HEARING REQUIRED. Petitioner must provide the Court with a Proof of Service showing that the Petition was served with 15 days notice of the May 30, 2019 hearing. Proof of Service only shows the Notice of Hearing was served. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: APIC HOTEL METROPOLIS LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. BLACK RABBIT HOSPITAL INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 18, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF APIC HOTEL METROPOLIS LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment HEARING REQUIRED. The Parties should come prepared to answer the following questions: What does Plaintiff seek to attach: The proceeds from the sale of the liquor license, the defendant's interest in the three ongoing business enterprises, or something else? Defendant seeks to exempt her Subaru. Exemption denied. This is not a necessity of life. The exemption for bank deposits is denied as to the first $1225.00. Anything in excess is subject to attachment. (CCP 704.080(b)(1).) Defendant does not explain why her employment income constitutes "earnings" as opposed to payments made from an owned business entity. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS VS. SAEED KHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Temporary Restraining Order And Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 18, 2019 line 9. PLAINTIFF CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Temporary Restraining Order And Preliminary Injunction HEARING REQUIRED. It is clear that Plaintiff is suffering irreparable harm. However, at this stage it is unclear whether Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits against the only current Defendants. In other words, it is unclear who is legally liable for the sewage based harm that is being caused. The lease suggests that the Landlord (not currently a party to this case) has the duty to "maintain and repair structural portions of the Premises[.]" (Lease, 6.02) However, the tenants to the lease are liable for "fixtures[.]" (Lease, 3.) It is not clear to the Court whether the sewage issues are due to a "structural" or "fixture" failure. Additionally, it appears that Defendant is a subtenant franchisee operator of the tenant Jack in the Box Inc. (also not currently a party to this case). Other issues include that Defendant has allegedly made an attempt to remediate the plumbing/sewage issue, and claims that Plaintiff has interfered with their ability to do so (unclean hands). Therefore some of the continued damages may be caused by Plaintiff its self.
